post_rendered:
Welcome to Mafia Game 191: Professional Jobbers Mafia
Rules
  • Rules
    • 1.1: Read the rules. All of them. Read them multiple times if necessary.
    • 1.2: Do not post at night.
    • 1.3: You may PM me at any time and ask me anything, such as rule clarification.
    • 1.4: Do not talk about this game outside of this topic to anyone other than the mods in charge, unless I specifically allow you to do so.
    • 1.5: You may not use any formatting that has an effect of concealing text. This includes size=1, hide="", color=#FFFFFF, and color=white, among others. This does not include adding extra spaces.
    • 1.6: Do not edit or delete posts you have already submitted; this includes editing right after you post (what goes in the thread stays in the thread). If you for any reason find the need to change your post, use EBWOP or EBWON as necessary.
    • 1.7: If you are not a living participant of the game, do not post anything else about the game anywhere. You may still spectate the game, but you may no longer post in this thread.
    • 1.8: Do not advocate for breaking the rules.
    • 1.9: Do not use the colors red (#FF0000), blue (#0000FF), green (#008000), or anything that appears similar, as these colors are used for modding purposes.
    • 1.10: Confirm by describing a time you were a professional jobber.
  • Voting/Hanging
    • 2.1: Post votes in bold font in the format "Vote: <insert player here>". Since AoPS does not distinguish between usernames with different capitalizations, the capitalization of the player name does not matter. For example, "Vote: fdas" and Vote: fDAs is an acceptable vote, while "Vote: fdas", "Vote fdas", “vote: fdas, Vote:fdas, Vote: fads, Vote: fdsa, and "Vote: fdas" are not.
    • 2.2: Votes may not be placed in hide tags.
    • 2.3: Improperly made votes will not be counted under any circumstances. It does not matter if it is the end of the game hammer and everyone revealed their roles. Hint: don’t reveal your roles and declare victory until I made the official end of the game post.
    • 2.4: You may vote for no hang to occur during a day. Vote this by including in your post "Vote: No Hang".
    • 2.5: You do not have to unvote before making a new vote, though it is appreciated if you do so. Unvote in the format "Unvote". An unvote will undo your previous vote, if any.
    • 2.6: Spell the username of the player you wish to vote for exactly. Do not use nicknames.
    • 2.7: If at any point in a day more than half of the votes are for a single player, that player will be hanged. It will then immediately be twilight.
    • 2.8: If at any point in a day at least half of the votes are for no hang, then there will be no hang. It will then be twilight.
    • 2.9: If the deadline is met and no person is hanged, it will be considered no hang and will proceed to be twilight.
    • 2.10: The moment the deadline passes, no more votes will be counted even if the mod has not made a post.
    • 2.11: During twilight, players may still post. However, no more votes and/or unvotes will be processed.
    • 2.12: Once I post a end-of-day scene, the person hanged (if any) is officially dead. Night then immediately begins. You may not post during night.

  • Activity
    • 3.1: Each day will be 336 hours (2 weeks). The moderator may extend the deadline for the end of the day at his/her discretion. Note that this will be unlikely.
    • 3.2: The deadlines will follow the official time
    • 3.3: The deadline will occur the very second 504 hours has occurred.
    • 3.4: The night deadline will be 72 hours after night begins.
    • 3.5: When the night deadline is met, if the moderator does not receive a night action from either a player or his/her partner(s) (if you have them), the player will lose the chance to perform an action during that night.
    • 3.6: Any night results will be sent after the night deadline
    • 3.7: Night will end at some time after the night deadline when the mod makes the post announcing the end of night.
    • 3.8: If a player does not post in a 48-hour period and he/she is not in V/LA, you will receive an in-game prod.
    • 3.9: If a player does not post in another 24-hour period after receiving an in-game prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will receive a PM prod.
    • 3.10: If a player does not post in another 48-hour period after receiving a PM prod and he/she is not in V/LA, he/she will be replaced, unless you post before the moderator finds a replacement.
    • 3.11: If a player receives 5 PM Prods, he/she will be replaced
    • 3.12: If a player is going to be V/LA, he/she must post in bold in-thread or PM me. The moderator will not prod the player unless the V/LA is too long.

  • Etiquette
    • 4.1: Do not use inappropriate language.
    • 4.2: Do not make any personal attacks.
    • 4.3: Follow all of the rules.
    • 4.4: Be mature.
    • 4.5: For a minor rule infraction, players can receive up to two warnings. If a player commits an infraction after he/she has run out of warnings, or commits a modkill level infraction, he/she will be modkilled or force replaced. Please do not make me modkill you.

  • Moderators/Co-Moderators
    • 5.1: I (fdas) am the moderator of Mafia Game 191
    • 5.2: Red-colored text (#FF0000) is used for official votecounts and activity checks by the moderators. It is also used for mafia flavor-related posts.
    • 5.3: Blue-colored text (#0000FF) is used for the moderator and co-mod's posts other than votecounts and activity checks.
    • 5.4: Green-colored text (#00FF00) is used for town flavor-related posts.
    • 5.5: The not voting section is to be ignored if it contradicts any other part of the votecount
    • 5.6: If an error to the votecount is not pointed out within 5 days, the incorrect votecount will become official.
    • 5.7: The moderator may change the rules at any time.
    • 5.8: The moderator has the last say in interpreting the rules.

  • Read all of the rules

Setup
7 Professional Jobbers
3 Mafia Goons
Quote:
You are a Professional Jobber (Town)
You are highly skilled at math however you have one flaw. You often perform silly mistakes on math problems such as adding wrong or having a random 1 come out of nowhere and make your answer wrong. You recall answering 8 questions on the AIME once and getting 3 correct.
You have 4 abilities: Cop, Doctor, Roleblocker, Vigilante
Every night you may use any combination of those abilities. However, the more abilities you use, the more likely it is for you to screw up.

1 ability used:
50% chance success
50% chance no action made

2 abilities used:
50% chance success
25% chance no action made
25% chance you target a random other person instead

3 abilities used
25% chance success
50% chance no action made
25% chance you target a random other person instead

4 abilities used
25% chance success
25% chance no action made
50% chance you target a random other person instead

Random.org will be used once to determine what happens. If it is a success, all actions succeed. If it is a fail, then all actions fail. If it is a hit random person, all actions will hit a random person which will be separately determined for each action. You win when there are only Professional Jobbers (Town) left in the game.


Alive (7)
benjaminsz
NDMath

lkarhat
kevinmathz
avanders SmartZX


Dead(3)
fdas (Professional Jobber) removed Night 0.
mathjay (Spy)
RTG (Professional Jobber)
litee (Spy)


Events
Day 1: No Hang
Night 1: No one dies
Day 2: mathjay hanged
Night 2: RTG, litee dies


PM Prods
 


Warnings
 


Story
fdas gazed outward toward the room in front of him. There were eleven people in the room. fdas was the captain of the Professional Jobbers PuMaC team and the people here were supposed to be the team members. There was only one issue. A PuMaC team is only supposed to have eight people! There were eleven people here total. So clearly three extras had made it in somehow. fdas tried to check who was supposed to be on the team, but it seems that the team roster had been misplaced. fdas couldn't remember which were the people who were supposed to be on the team.

"I have discovered something very disturbing," fdas said. "Three spies have infiltrated our PuMaC team. I don't know what they are here to do, but they probably mean to sabotage us somehow."

The room broke out into chaos. Accusations were being shouted out by everyone.

"Silence!" fdas shouted. "Remain calm. I will deal with this situation. Rest assured, by the end of the day I will have figured out a way to deal with this."

The room quieted down. Everyone hoped that this could be resolved quickly and effectively.

Suddenly, the door opened and the math coach entered. "fdas, I have received some very disturbing reports about you. It seems that evidence has emerged that you are very incompetent at math. You are clearly not fit to be on the PuMaC team, let alone lead it."

fdas and everyone else in the room was shocked into silence. fdas's typical eloquence failed him, but as he was taken out of the room he stood motionless, impassive, unconquerable, coolly defying them all.

After fdas's removal, the room fell into silence. The once calm Professional Jobbers looked at each other in suspicion, in fear of who would be next. It was clear that the spies must be rooted out, before they were all doomed.


Do not post until this line is crossed out.

Day 1 begins. Current time: 11:00:00 EST 9 November 2019.

Laws of Cop Checks
T = Conf Town
M = Conf Mafia
? = Unknown Allignment
t = Town Check
m = Mafia Check
A, B, etc. = Players

1. TA(tB) = TB
2. TA(mB) = MB
3. ?A(tD)+?B(tD)+?C(tD) = TD
4. ?A(tC)+?B(tC)+?C(tD) = TD
5. ?A(tB)+?B(tC)+?C(tD) = TD
6. ?A(mB) = (MB OR MA)

After typing that out I realize it is less useful than it was in my head.


Also people should consider doctoring the person you cop to increase the expected number of checks per night. And because of that don't share who you plan to cop.

post_rendered:
Clarification post:
There seems to be some confusion over how the Professional Jobber role works.

Quote:
Random.org will be used once to determine what happens. If it is a success, all actions succeed. If it is a fail, then all actions fail. If it is a hit random person, all actions will hit a random person which will be separately determined for each action.

If an action that returns a result fails, it will return no result. Out of the four actions, only the cop action returns a result (either Town or Mafia). Results returned by an action is the only feedback you will receive from your night action.

Also people should consider doctoring the person you cop to increase the expected number of checks per night. And because of that don't share who you plan to cop.

There are three mafia so we’d technically need 4 TRs to prove them inno. Actually I got a strat that might work. We all target one person with our cop checks first night and then protect randomly and not give that info. Then we’ll get returns the next day. If 50% or more returns are scum, we hang that person, if 20% or below we trust them as town. If between undecided and may or may not hang them.

Here’s my reasoning: there’ll be 6 or 7 PJs tomorrow and 3 mafia let’s say 6. If the 6 of us target one person approx 3 of us will get the actual result of that person and 1 to 2 will get a role of another person which is a 67% chance of being town. Add in the 3 mafia who want to throw us off. So let’s say we target a town. Now the mafia could all say they got mafia on him but that could give themselves away too easily. 2-3 of us approx. would get town, and 1 to 2 would get town 67% of the time so let’s say for conservative estimates we get 3 town returns and 1 maf return from the PJs. The mafia could then push the hang saying they all got mafia returns. However that would put all of them in hot water for getting hung next.

Now say we target a mafia. We get the PJ returns again and say now 3 mafia returns and 1 town return. The mafia could say he’s town and bring it to 3 town returns but we still end up hanging him. That would again put the 2 remaining mafia in hot water of being hung next.

Now if the mafia end up cooperating with town we get a conf town or a conf scum which are both hella useful and then if the person we check is conf town we could use whoever he targeted that night as a lead. We can also get hints as to whether we targeted someone random if the person you protect dies that night so we can avoid using your cop read that day.

At a little less than a week before the EOD (We have 3 week days) we have a community readlist which is basically a player most people think is pretty townie ranks the players by what they think general opinion of scummiest to towniest is.

Most Suspicious
Is hung today.

#2 Susp
Is "Primary suspect"
Cops "Secondary suspect"
Doctors player of their choice.

#3 Susp
Is "Secondary Suspect"
Cops "Primary Suspect"
Does not Doctor

3 mid/null reads
Cops "Primary Suspect"
Does not Doctor

4 towniest
Cops "Secondary Suspect"
Each doctors player of their choice.

We want the four towniest players to be on the cop-Doctor committee because we want town members to doctor so they actually happen. Actually I just came up with a different idea. NDs strat could end up getting us to LyLo immediately with only having info on one mafia. Here’s what I think.

We choose 2 suspects. Then we assign 5 to inspect each suspect. Everyone only cops so we get an accurate results so we will lose one townie but we don’t get mafia being able to defend themselves by saying it was the random mechanic. Then majority rules on whether the guy is good or bad. The people who got guilty on their suspect claim first, then after that is done the people who got inno claim next. If two get inno and one get guilty we hang the one person who said guilty. If that guy ends up being town we have the entire mafia team with the suspect and the two that said inno. If 2 say guilty and 1 says inno, the mafia are idiots lolol cause then we hang the suspect and then have a second mafia or the two that said guilty are mafia. If 1 says guilty and 1 says inno, we hang the one that says guilty cause we either hang the one mafia and get two proven townie, or we hang a townie, but have knowledge of two mafia. If we get two inno and two guilty, we hang one of the two that said guilty cause then we’d have all 3 mafia if that guy flips town.

This optimizes all situations for us by finding as many mafia as possible for as few of our members. Also ND there is 0 point to hanging anyone today when we got so many cop reports.

The one thing I got questioning it though is that the more we spread our resources, the more likely it is for us to not get any result on a player. Also if one calls the suspect inno, a mafia in that group might think it’s useful giving himself up by going to LyLo by saying the suspect is guilty so if this happens I suggest hanging the person who said guilty there first because either we’re not going to be at LyLo the next day with a mafia hang, or we’ll be at LyLo with two different mafia confirmed.
We don't need the two main checks to check eachother because we have enough people doing that, and we only need a single confirm town to check someone successfully to know their alignment.

Edit to the plan to make it more effective.
{A, B, C, D, E, F, G, H, I, J}
A is checked by C, E, G, I
B is checked by D, F, H, J
A checks D
B checks C
The effect of this is that in the rare event where A and B are both town, and the mafia kill neither, and the mafia don't try any stunts and both of the checks succeed, we get an extra conf town.

NDMath wrote:
Laws of Cop Checks
T = Conf Town
M = Conf Mafia
? = Unknown Allignment
t = Town Check
m = Mafia Check
A, B, etc. = Players

1. TA(tB) = TB
2. TA(mB) = MB
3. ?A(tD)+?B(tD)+?C(tD) = TD
4. ?A(tC)+?B(tC)+?C(tD) = TD
5. ?A(tB)+?B(tC)+?C(tD) = TD
6. ?A(mB) = (MB OR MA)

After typing that out I realize it is less useful than it was in my head.


Also people should consider doctoring the person you cop to increase the expected number of checks per night. And because of that don't share who you plan to cop.

There are three mafia so we’d technically need 4 TRs to prove them inno. Actually I got a strat that might work. We all target one person with our cop checks first night and then protect randomly and not give that info. Then we’ll get returns the next day. If 50% or more returns are scum, we hang that person, if 20% or below we trust them as town. If between undecided and may or may not hang them.

Here’s my reasoning: there’ll be 6 or 7 PJs tomorrow and 3 mafia let’s say 6. If the 6 of us target one person approx 3 of us will get the actual result of that person and 1 to 2 will get a role of another person which is a 67% chance of being town. Add in the 3 mafia who want to throw us off. So let’s say we target a town. Now the mafia could all say they got mafia on him but that could give themselves away too easily. 2-3 of us approx. would get town, and 1 to 2 would get town 67% of the time so let’s say for conservative estimates we get 3 town returns and 1 maf return from the PJs. The mafia could then push the hang saying they all got mafia returns. However that would put all of them in hot water for getting hung next.

Now say we target a mafia. We get the PJ returns again and say now 3 mafia returns and 1 town return. The mafia could say he’s town and bring it to 3 town returns but we still end up hanging him. That would again put the 2 remaining mafia in hot water of being hung next.

Now if the mafia end up cooperating with town we get a conf town or a conf scum which are both hella useful and then if the person we check is conf town we could use whoever he targeted that night as a lead. We can also get hints as to whether we targeted someone random if the person you protect dies that night so we can avoid using your cop read that day.

Clarification post:
There seems to be some confusion over how the Professional Jobber role works.

Quote:
Random.org will be used once to determine what happens. If it is a success, all actions succeed. If it is a fail, then all actions fail. If it is a hit random person, all actions will hit a random person which will be separately determined for each action.

We all target one person with our cop checks first night and then protect randomly and not give that info.  Then we’ll get returns the next day.  If 50% or more returns are scum, we hang that person, if 20% or below we trust them as town.  If between undecided and may or may not hang them.

Here’s my reasoning: there’ll be 6 or 7 PJs tomorrow and 3 mafia let’s say 6.  If the 6 of us target one person approx 3 of us will get the actual result of that person and 1 to 2 will get a role of another person which is a 67% chance of being town. The mafia could then push the hang saying they all got mafia returns.  However that would put all of them in hot water for getting hung next.

Now say we target a mafia.  We get the PJ returns again and say now 3 mafia returns and 1 town return.  The mafia could say he’s town and bring it to 3 town returns but we still end up hanging him.  That would again put the 2 remaining mafia in hot water of being hung next.

There seems to be some confusion over how the Professional Jobber role works.
[/b]

[quote]Random.org will be used once to determine what happens. If it is a success, all actions succeed. If it is a fail, then all actions fail. If it is a hit random person, all actions will hit a random person which will be separately determined for each action.

Given the Ben and My2 cop checks it is safe to say mathjay is mafia. From my above post lkar is town and either way their result doesn't matter due to the three innocents on me.

Before I say my check I'll explain my process to get their. Before the EOD1 I told mathjay to cop in the opposite group because I felt like {RTG, litee, Ben, MY2} were all good choices for me to check. RTG townread by mostly everyone, litee null read by mostly everyone, Ben scum read by mostly everyone, and MY2 read differently by people. I then did some math to determine which would be most beneficial to cop check. Variable p' represents the chance the checked player was town before the check and Variable p" represents the chance the checked player was town after the check. I can then form two equations, one if I get a town check and one of I get a mafia check.

Town Check: (24p')/(7+17p')=p"
Mafia Check: (3p')/(20-17p')=p"

I could make these equations because the cop check did not change the players alignment. A null read is 0.714 as from your perspective you+me are town and Jay is mafia. When I plug in a high value, for RTG, the town check baretly changes the number, and the mafia check brings him to roughly null, which isn't the most useful as we won't hang on a null read. When you plug in a low number, for Ben, the mafia check doesn't do much in that he goes from scummy to a bit more scummy. A town check only brings him up to roughly null, which isn't enough to guaranteedly stop a hang on him. Plugging in a number around .7, for litee, however, makes both checks have meaning. I town read my2 more than litee so I checked litee. And got Guilty.

I doctored myself and copped litee guilty. So either litee is mafia, RNG is favorable or both.

When I reread the thread RTG and myself were the only people who pushed for mathjay being copped, therefore I think we can say he's town.

Looks like mathjay is going to be annoying. We will hang him today, but before we do that we must discuss night actions.

The Professional Jobbers waited nervously in the large math classroom. Then, the math coach walked in. She was holding some papers.

"Last night, someone sent me an email containing a very disturbing video. In it RTG was saying that he was very bad at math. Moreover, I received a package containing one of RTGs tests. In it he failed multiple basic addition and multiplication computations. He also made such grievous as adding divergent series, overcounting combinations, and writing that the centroid is formed by the intersection of perpendicular bisectors. It is clear that RTG has no place on this team."

Everyone gasped in shock. Then, the math couch continued speaking.

"I also received one of litee's tests. In it he did almost two thirds of the problems incorrectly, making trivial mistakes. In some cases he even answered a completely different question than what was asked. It is clear that litee does not belong on the team either.

Then, the math coach left, taking a fearful RTG and litee away in tow. RTG was a Professional Jobber. litee was a Spy

post_rendered:
End of Day Votecount:
SmartZX (4 HANGED): NDMath, benjaminsz, zdtsd, MY-2


With the loss of RTG, the Professional Jobbers became even more frantic to find out who the spy in their midst was. Then, after a series of deductions, it was deduced that SmartZX was the final spy. Finally, SmartZX was expelled from the team. He was a Spy
Story
With the spies eliminated from the PUMaC team, the Professional Jobbers could let out a sigh of relief. The great crisis had passed. But then, they soon realized they had other problems. Now, their team only had 6 people, and they needed 8 for the contest. Everyone gathered in contemplation to determine what to do next.

"Maybe we can recruit some random students from the school to fill in for our team," NDMath said.

"Well, then our team has even less of a chance to do well," someone else replied. "It would be detrimental if we brought in random people onto our team with little math background...."

Everyone's eyes slowly drifted to a particular person.

"Err... any more random people, I mean."

The Professional Jobbers weren't sure what to do, so they decided to think more about it. Several days passed, with no one having any better ideas.

Then, one person had a thought. Quickly, he got everyone back together to discuss. "I just thought of something. Why do we have to listen to the math coach anyways. She doesn't actually run the PUMaC team."

"Huh?" One freshman was confused. "What do you mean? Isn't she the math coach?"

"Yes, she manages the other math activities like the AMCs and stuff, but our PUMaC team isn't actually school sponsored. We just go by ourselves. There is a math teacher that tags along to observe, but he isn't actually involved."

Silence permeated through the room.

"You mean to say, the math coach doesn't actually matter, and she doesn't decide who can be on the team? Wait... How was the team decided in the first place. I don't remember tryouts."

"Tryouts? If there were enough people for us to hold tryouts, we wouldn't have started conscripting people."

"So, if the math coach isn't in charge of the team, who is then?"

"The one with the password to the team's account that we registered with."

"Who is that?"

Suddenly, everyone fell silent.

"It's fdas..."

"Wait, how did he get kicked out of his own team then?"

***

Soon, everyone went to look for fdas, who was sitting in the library, on the computer. On the screen was the Art of Problem Solving website. Clearly, he was hard at work studying mathematics.

"fdas, we found out who the spies were."

"mathjay, litee, and SmartZX right?" fdas replied.

"How did you know?"

"I opened up our team list and looked at the names."

"Wait, why didn't you say this earlier. Also, didn't you know that you are the one with access to the account and the math coach can't remove you from the team?"

"Oh yeah. I was wondering when you guys would find out. I wanted to enjoy some peace and quiet rather than our scheduled team practices. Now I get to spend my time playing mafia online."

Everyone suddenly looked at fdas's computer screen. It appeared that he wasn't actually doing math on AoPS.

The disgruntled team members looked back. "Well, now that this is cleared up, we have to go back to doing math. Oh, and someone get RTG back here."

And so, the Professional Jobbers, the entire 8 person team, attended PUMaC. Entering in high spirits, they proceeded to begin taking the exam. After the individual round, they started discussing the exam. It appears that many of them made silly mistakes, and many easy questions were answered incorrectly. Somewhat depressed by this poor result, everyone then moved onto the team round.

The team round started off tense. Everyone read the special twist involved, and then they started strategizing. Unfortunately, there was little that they could decide on. Even fdas seemed to not have any good ideas on how to take advantage of the structure of the exam.

The Professional Jobbers started doing the Team Round questions. After the first ten minutes, most of the easy problems had been done, and they moved onto the harder problems. The problems were challenging, and little progress was being made. Twenty minutes in, someone discovered that problem 1 had been done incorrectly. Everyone quickly scrambled to check their work. As the errors piled up, and the answers were corrected, only for the corrections the be incorrect as well, things started looking bad.

Thirty minutes in, fdas, who had found himself unable to solve any more problems, started suffering a nervous breakdown. The only intelligible sounds that came out of his mouth was a sequence of numbers: 2, 2, 22, 22, 52. Nobody could figure out what this meant. There was no clear link to any of the problems.

Finally, the team round ended, and by the time they left, the team was too exhausted to talk. Only after they reached the student center, and got their dry sandwiches and overly large cookies, did they finally start to talk while they ate. They talked about the various mistakes they made during the exam.

Then, the individual finalist results were released. Against all odds, somehow fdas made it into the individual finals.

"Dang, it looks like I can't play board games now," fdas said.

And so, fdas disappeared for the next few hours, returning only when the live round was to start. When he arrived, he saw two other people on his team there.

"Where is everyone else?" fdas asked.

"They left," one of the few remaining replied.

"Well, its not like any of the rest would have helped much anyways. Except maybe as a runner."

After the live round, there was a short break, after which awards were to begin. The time for awards arrived... and nothing happened.

15 minutes later, a PUMaC organizer announced that there would be a short delay. 30 minutes later, there was still nothing.

Finally, the PUMaC organizer came up and said that they would be done soon. Then, he asked if anyone would like to tell some jokes to pass the time.

fdas went on stage. "So, there's a man crawling through the desert..."

Looking at his phone, fdas began to read the joke. About a minute into the joke, the organizer, who has spotted the size of the scroll bar on the side of the screen, whispered to fdas during a pause.

"Isn't this going to take an hour..."

fdas turned and told the PUMaC organizer, "That's how long it will take before the awards are actually ready."

***

Finally, the awards began. The Professional Jobbers did quite poorly, as expected. Everyone went home happily. THE END.

"Well, its not like any of the rest would have helped much anyways. Except maybe as a runner."

After the live round, there was a short break, after which awards were to begin. The time for awards arrived... and nothing happened.

15 minutes later, a PUMaC organizer announced that there would be a short delay. 30 minutes later, there was still nothing.

Finally, the PUMaC organizer came up and said that they would be done soon. Then, he asked if anyone would like to tell some jokes to pass the time.

fdas went on stage. "So, there's a man crawling through the desert..."

Looking at his phone, fdas began to read the joke. About a minute into the joke, the organizer, who has spotted the size of the scroll bar on the side of the screen, whispered to fdas during a pause.

"Isn't this going to take an hour..."

fdas turned and told the PUMaC organizer, "That's how long it will take before the awards are actually ready."

***

Finally, the awards began. The Professional Jobbers did quite poorly, as expected. Everyone went home happily. THE END.[/hide]

post_rendered:
Post game reflection:

This game was partially made to further explore the Professional Jobber role, to see how it would be used, since in previous games the PJ has sort of been underwhelming. It was an experimental setup to explore the dynamics of the role.

At first I had wanted to put in 4 mafia into the game. But I thought that the town would try to vig, and we would end up with 4 people dead in one night. With 4 mafia, that would be day 2 loss of majority. Put it seems that town, especially NDMath, had good foresight and was able to convince the town to follow a plan rather than randomly doing things. Faith in AoPS Mafia restored.
